Supreme Team.description: Organized crime syndicate based in South Jamaica, Queens, New York City, founded in 1981 by Kenneth 'Supreme' McGriff and his nephew Gerald 'Prince' Miller. Specialized in crack cocaine distribution, generating over $200,000 daily at its 1987 peak. Known for extreme violence and murder to control drug trade. Leaders were convicted and sentenced to life in prison.

descriptions: Supreme Team is listed as a rival of South Brooklyn Boys in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: The Jokers, Untouchable Bishops, The Mau Maus, The Fort Green Chaplains, The West Street Boys, Supreme Team

descriptions: Supreme Team listed Black Disciples as allies in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lorenzo Nichols gang, Crips, Black Disciples

descriptions: Supreme Team listed Crips as allies in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lorenzo Nichols gang, Crips, Black Disciples

descriptions: Supreme Team was allied with and supplied cocaine by Lorenzo Nichols gang.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1987, the Supreme Team was allied with another drug gang, led by Lorenzo "Fat Cat" Nichols, who supplied the Supreme Team with powder cocaine.

descriptions: Supreme Team listed South Brooklyn Boys as rivals in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: South Brooklyn Boys

Clan Pecoraro-Renna.description: The Pecoraro-Renna clan is a Camorra clan originating from Battipaglia, Italy. Founded by Giovanni Pecoraro after his release from prison, it fought against the Cutolo and De Feo clans in the Sele Plain. After Giovanni's murder in 1988, his brothers Alfonso and Francesco took over, leading a bloody feud. The clan is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, usury, prostitution, and tax evasion, with operations in the Sele Plain and surrounding areas.

descriptions: Cesarano clan allied with Pecoraro-Renna clan.
edges.evidence_quote: the clan had made alliances with ... the Pecoraro-Renna clan from Battipaglia

descriptions: Cesarano formed alliances with Mallardo and Pecoraro-Renna clans.
edges.evidence_quote: il clan aveva stretto alleanze con i clan Mallardo di Giugliano in Campania e Pecoraro-Renna di Battipaglia

descriptions: The Pecoraro-Renna clan is supported by the Bisogni family.
edges.evidence_quote: sostenute da altre famiglie criminali, come i Bisogni di Bellizzi ed i Mogavero di Pontecagnano Faiano

descriptions: The Pecoraro-Renna clan clashed with the De Feo clan.
edges.evidence_quote: I Pecoraro-Renna si scontrarono con il clan De Feo di Bellizzi, appartenente alla Nuova Camorra Organizzata.

descriptions: After conflict, the Pecoraro-Renna and De Feo clans formed an alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Dopo i pesanti contrasti, i Pecoraro-Renna ed i De Feo hanno stretto una alleanza

descriptions: The Pecoraro-Renna clan was supported by the Maiale family.
edges.evidence_quote: insieme alla famiglia Renna e con l'appoggio della potente famiglia Maiale di Eboli

descriptions: The Frappaolo family split from the Pecoraro-Renna clan.
edges.evidence_quote: All'interno del clan si sarebbero verificate delle scissioni, a séguito delle quali sarebbero sorti due gruppi autonomi facenti capo alle famiglie Trimarco, Frappaolo e Giffoni.

descriptions: The Giffoni family split from the Pecoraro-Renna clan.
edges.evidence_quote: All'interno del clan si sarebbero verificate delle scissioni, a séguito delle quali sarebbero sorti due gruppi autonomi facenti capo alle famiglie Trimarco, Frappaolo e Giffoni.

descriptions: The Pecoraro-Renna clan is supported by the Mogavero family.
edges.evidence_quote: sostenute da altre famiglie criminali, come i Bisogni di Bellizzi ed i Mogavero di Pontecagnano Faiano

descriptions: Giovanni Pecoraro allied with Pasquale Galasso, boss of the Nuova Famiglia.
edges.evidence_quote: Giovanni Pecoraro figura storica del clan, conobbe in carcere Pasquale Galasso , boss del clan omonimo e della Nuova Famiglia , e ad egli si alleò.

descriptions: The Trimarco family split from the Pecoraro-Renna clan.
edges.evidence_quote: All'interno del clan si sarebbero verificate delle scissioni, a séguito delle quali sarebbero sorti due gruppi autonomi facenti capo alle famiglie Trimarco, Frappaolo e Giffoni.

descriptions: Pecoraro-Renna clan was part of the anti-Cutolo alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Altre famiglie coinvolte nell'ampia alleanza anticutoliana furono ... i Pecoraro-Renna di Battipaglia

descriptions: Cárteles Unidos lists Los Blancos de Troya as allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: La Familia Michoacana , Los Viagras , La Nueva Familia Michoacana , Los Blancos de Troya , Los Correa

descriptions: Blancos de Troya is a faction of Cárteles Unidos.
edges.evidence_quote: Facciones que la conforman: Blancos de Troya

descriptions: Los Blancos De La Troya is listed as an ally of Los Viagras.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: La Nueva Familia Michoacana, Los Blancos De La Troya, Guardia Michoacana, Cártel Independiente de Acapulco (defunct), Beltrán-Leyva Cartel (defunct)

edges.evidence_quote: siendo comandante del brazo armado "Los Blancos de Troya" Los Viagras los meses posteriores.

Shower Posse.description: The Shower Posse is a Jamaican gang founded around 1981 by Lester Lloyd Coke and Vivian Blake, based in Tivoli Gardens, Kingston. It is involved in drug and arms smuggling, with branches in North America and the United Kingdom. The gang has ties to the Jamaica Labour Party and has been linked to CIA support. It remains active in drug trafficking, firearms trafficking, and other criminal activities.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Shower Posse as a rival of the Black Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Shower Posse , and various other gangs in the Philadelphia area, including their allies

descriptions: Ghetto Boys allegedly had links with Shower Posse.
edges.evidence_quote: It is alleged the group had links with the Jamaican gang Shower Posse

descriptions: Infoboox lists Shower Posse as ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Shower Posse

descriptions: JBM fought a drug war against the Shower Posse.
edges.evidence_quote: The JBM was involved in a drug war with the Shower Posse in Southwest Philadelphia during the 1980s and early 1990s.

descriptions: The Dog Posse challenged the Shower Posse for control of operations.
edges.evidence_quote: The Dog Posse challenged the Shower Posse for control of operations in New Jersey and Philadelphia.

descriptions: The Shower Posse was involved in a drug war with the Junior Black Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: The Shower Posse was involved in a drug war with the Junior Black Mafia in Southwest Philadelphia during the 1980s and early 1990s.

descriptions: The Shower Posse fought against the Tel Aviv Posse.
edges.evidence_quote: elements of the Shower Posse and Spangler Posse from Brooklyn and the Bronx fought with the Boston-based Dog Posse and Tel Aviv Posse.

descriptions: Shower Syndicate allegedly had links with Shower Posse.
edges.evidence_quote: It is alleged the group had links with the Jamaican gang Shower Posse.

descriptions: Vagabonds attended Satan's Choice's national convention in 1967.
edges.evidence_quote: Guindon held the first national convention of Satan's Choice at a farmhouse in Markham Township just outside of Toronto, which was also attended by the Vagabonds club.

descriptions: Satan's Choice worked closely with Vagabonds to manufacture and distribute methamphetamine.
edges.evidence_quote: A report by the police Organized Crime Committee from 1989 stated that Satan's Choice, the Vagabonds and the Lobos were working closely to manufacture and distribute methamphetamine.

'Ndrina Albanese.description: The Albanese are a powerful 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cittanova and Laureana di Borrello, Calabria. They have branches in Brescia, Tuscany, Lazio, and Liguria. Their activities include drug trafficking, grape market control, and past kidnappings. They are allied with the Raso-Gullace clan and have been involved in feuds with other clans.

descriptions: Bellocco, along with Mancuso, Pesce, and Piromalli, mediated peace for the Albanese in the Laureana di Borrello feud.
edges.evidence_quote: si risolve con una pace voluta dai Mancuso - Pesce - Piromalli e Bellocco

descriptions: Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone clashed with Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni Novanta scoppia la faida di Laureana di Borrello tra gli Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone e i Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino

descriptions: Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone clashed with Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni Novanta scoppia la faida di Laureana di Borrello tra gli Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone e i Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino

descriptions: Albanese, together with Raso and Gullace, clashed with Facchineri in the 1960s.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni sessanta a Cittanova insieme ai Raso e ai Gullace si sono scontrati con i Facchineri , i Marvaso e i Monteleone.

descriptions: The Albanese are part of the Gullace-Raso-Albanese group targeted in Operation Alchemia.
edges.evidence_quote: Con la conclusione dell'operazione Alchemia della DDA di Reggio Calabria e dello SCO di Roma del 19 luglio 2016 vengono arrestate 40 persone tra la Calabria e la Liguria presunte affiliate dei Gullace-Raso-Albanese e Parrello-Gagliostro

edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni sessanta a Cittanova insieme ai Raso e ai Gullace si sono scontrati con i Facchineri , i Marvaso e i Monteleone.

edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni Novanta scoppia la faida di Laureana di Borrello tra gli Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone e i Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino

edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni Novanta scoppia la faida di Laureana di Borrello tra gli Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone e i Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino

edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni Novanta scoppia la faida di Laureana di Borrello tra gli Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone e i Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino

descriptions: Facchineri and Albanese were in conflict in the Cittanova feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Cittanova : originatasi probabilmente nel 1964 quando venne ucciso un parente della 'ndrina dei Facchineri i quali risposero uccidendo un membro degli Albanese.

descriptions: Facchineri and Albanese were in conflict in the second Cittanova feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Seconda faida di Cittanova : emerge dal 1987 e termina nel 1991 con la vittoria dei Facchineri sui Raso-Albanese-Gullace.

descriptions: Facchineri-Varone were in conflict with Albanese in the 1960s faida.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni sessanta scoppia la prima faida fra i Facchineri-Varone(alleati ai Marvaso e Monteleone) e i Raso, gli Albanese , i Gullace e i De Raco.

Ghost Shadows.description: The Ghost Shadows was a Chinese American street gang prominent in New York City's Chinatown from the early 1970s to the mid 1990s. Formed in 1971 by immigrants from Taiwan and Hong Kong, it was affiliated with the On Leong Tong. The gang engaged in extortion, kidnapping, murder, racketeering, drug trafficking, and illegal gambling. It became defunct due to federal RICO crackdowns in the 1990s.

descriptions: Born to Kill was in a violent conflict with Ghost Shadows over turf.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was also once embroiled in a violent conflict with the Chinese Ghost Shadows over turf of the lucrative activities.

descriptions: The Flying Dragons fought turf wars with the Ghost Shadows in the 1980s.
edges.evidence_quote: During the 1980s, the gang often engaged in bloody turf wars with the newer Ghost Shadows gang.

descriptions: The Flying Dragons had the Ghost Shadows as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Born to Kill , Ghost Shadows

descriptions: Big Circle Gang is listed as an ally of Ghost Shadows in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: On Leong Tong , Big Circle Gang , Tiny Rascal Gang

descriptions: Ghost Shadows fought turf wars with Division Street Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: the gang was often engaged in bloody turf wars with other Chinatown gangs such as the older Flying Dragons, affiliated with Hip Sing Tong and the Division Street Boys affiliated with Tung On Association.

descriptions: Ghost Shadows fought turf wars with Flying Dragons.
edges.evidence_quote: the gang was often engaged in bloody turf wars with other Chinatown gangs such as the older Flying Dragons

descriptions: Ghost Shadows was affiliated with On Leong Tong.
edges.evidence_quote: the gang was affiliated with the On Leong Tong

descriptions: Ghost Shadows had links to Sicilian-American Mafia families.
edges.evidence_quote: The Ghost Shadows' influence was widespread, having links to Chinatowns in other cities, as well as links to Sicilian-American Mafia families.

descriptions: Tiny Rascal Gang is listed as an ally of Ghost Shadows in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: On Leong Tong , Big Circle Gang , Tiny Rascal Gang

edges.evidence_quote: the Tong has been linked to the Ghost Shadows street gang led by Wing Yeung Chan

edges.evidence_quote: They have formed alliances with Chinese organizations such as the 14K , Wah Ching and Wo Hop To in California and the New York City -based Ghost Shadows .

edges.evidence_quote: The White Tigers did have territory in upper Chinatown in Manhattan, on Mott Street, with permission from the Ghost Shadows

descriptions: The Bidognetti faction allied with Schiavone against De Falco.

edges.evidence_quote: I contrasti tra gli Schiavone e i De Falco

descriptions: The Uda Organization's leader started as a foot soldier in the Hariri Organization.
edges.evidence_quote: Koteir Uda began his criminal career as a foot soldier in the Hariri organization, a prominent crime family in the Israeli Arab community centered in Tayibe.

descriptions: Members of the Renegades MC shot and killed an Outlaws member in a gunfight.
edges.evidence_quote: Frank Rego Vital of Roberta, an Outlaws member, was shot and killed in an early morning gunfight on June 24, 2007, in the parking lot of The Crazy Horse Saloon strip club in Forest Park by two members of the Renegades MC

Clan Nuvoletta.description: Italian mafia clan based in Marano di Napoli, founded in the 1960s by the Nuvoletta brothers Angelo, Lorenzo, and Ciro. It was one of the most powerful clans between the 1970s and 1990s, involved in cigarette smuggling and heroin trafficking, with strong ties to Sicilian Cosa Nostra. After the deaths of its historical leaders in the 1990s-2000s, it was replaced by the Polverino and Orlando clans.

descriptions: Infobox lists Nuvoletta clan as rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Nuvoletta clan

descriptions: Nuvoletta started a war with the Alfieri faction after the split.
edges.evidence_quote: I Nuvoletta iniziano una guerra con i fuoriusciti della Nuova Famiglia che appoggiavano Carmine Alfieri

descriptions: Nuvoletta clan is a constituent clan of the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: Examples include the Nuvoletta clan, from Marano di Napoli, named after the Nuvoletta brothers: Lorenzo, Angelo and Ciro;

descriptions: The Nuvoletta are a Camorra clan strongly tied to Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan Nuvoletta di Marano, fortemente intrecciato con Cosa nostra, consolidò un profilo di potere

descriptions: Raffaele Abbinante was originally an affiliate of the Nuvoletta clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Raffaele Abbinante, detto “Papele e Marano”, nasce come affiliato al clan Nuvoletta

descriptions: Bardellino and Nuvoletta clans fought a war after NF disintegrated.
edges.evidence_quote: a war breaking out between the Bardellino and Nuvoletta clans towards the end of 1983

edges.evidence_quote: The internal infighting between the Casalesi and Nuvoletta clans eventually culminated in the massacre at Torre Annunziata

descriptions: Clan Nuvoletta was part of Nuova Famiglia fighting against NCO.
edges.evidence_quote: La guerra è combattuta dai cutoliani e dagli affiliati della Nuova Famiglia in cui, oltre al clan di Bardellino e quello dei Nuvoletta

descriptions: The Nuvoletta-Gionta alliance fought against the rest of the Nuova Famiglia from 1983 to 1985.
edges.evidence_quote: faida all'interno della Nuova Famiglia : combattuta tra i Nuvoletta - Gionta e la Nuova Famiglia (Alfieri, Galasso, Fabbrocino, Bardellino e Vastarella) negli anni 1983-85

descriptions: Clan Nuvoletta was a member of the Nuova Famiglia coalition.
edges.evidence_quote: La guerra è combattuta dai cutoliani e dagli affiliati della Nuova Famiglia in cui, oltre al clan di Bardellino e quello dei Nuvoletta

descriptions: The Nuvoletta clan was a member of the Nuova Famiglia federation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Nuvoletta clan first began to appear in the news at the same time the Nuova Famiglia (NF) was formed.

descriptions: Polverino replaced Clan Nuvoletta and has strong family ties to it.
edges.evidence_quote: il clan è stato sostituito sul territorio dai Polverino e Orlando , entrambi con forti legami di parentela con i Nuvoletta.

descriptions: The Polverino clan succeeded the Nuvoletta clan after its decline.
edges.evidence_quote: the clan was succeeded by the Polverino and Orlando clans

descriptions: Both were members of the Nuova Famiglia federation.
edges.evidence_quote: the Nuova Famiglia (NF) was formed. The NF was a federation of Camorra clans which apart from the Nuovoletta clan, consisted of ... the Vollaro clan

descriptions: The Nuvoletta clan was loyal to the Corleonesi during the Second Mafia War.
edges.evidence_quote: La faida si allargò anche al napoletano e vide contrapposti il clan Nuvoletta di Marano di Napoli , fedele ai Corleonesi

descriptions: Corleonesi allied with Nuvoletta, Lubrano-Ligato, and Gionta.
edges.evidence_quote: Elementi di spicco dei Corleonesi ... si allearono con i clan camorristici dei Nuvoletta , Lubrano-Ligato e Gionta

descriptions: The NCO fought against Nuvoletta and other entrepreneurial cartels.
time_periods: fine di questo decennio e i primissimi anni di quello successivo (late 1970s-early 1980s)
edges.evidence_quote: Gli scontri tra NCO e cartelli “imprenditoriali” (Zaza, Nuvoletta, Bardellino e alleati)

descriptions: NF included Nuvoletta clan initially, but later war broke out between Bardellino (NF leader) and Nuvoletta.
edges.evidence_quote: the NF alliance soon disintegrated, with a war breaking out between the Bardellino and Nuvoletta clans

descriptions: After defeating the NCO, the Nuova Famiglia split into factions, with the Nuvoletta clan opposing the Bardellino faction.
edges.evidence_quote: Da una parte i Bardellino e le altre fedelissime famiglie e dall'altra i Nuvoletta

descriptions: Nuova Famiglia allied with the Nuvoletta clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Successivamente la FN si alleò alle cosche e ai boss vicini alla mafia siciliana, tra cui la famiglia Nuvoletta

descriptions: The Nuvoletta clan was part of the Nuova Famiglia alliance.
time_periods: tra fine di questo decennio e i primissimi anni di quello successivo (late 1970s-early 1980s)
edges.evidence_quote: Proprio tra fine di questo decennio e i primissimi anni di quello successivo nacque, “d’intesa con Cosa nostra”, la Nuova Famiglia (NF) — asse Bardellino-Nuvoletta-Alfieri

descriptions: Nuvoletta joined the Nuova Famiglia.
edges.evidence_quote: Quando nella Nuova Famiglia subentrarono anche i Nuvoletta , gli Alfieri , i Galasso , i Misso della Sanità e soprattutto i Casalesi

descriptions: The Nuvoletta clan was part of the Nuova Famiglia confederation.
edges.evidence_quote: The confederation included: - Lorenzo, Angelo and Ciro Nuvoletta, bosses of the Nuvoletta clan

descriptions: Polverino clan is the successor of the Nuvoletta clan.
edges.evidence_quote: According to the investigations, the Polverinos are considered the successors of the Nuvoletta clan.

descriptions: Polverino clan originated as a ramification of the Nuvoletta clan and later surpassed them.
edges.evidence_quote: The clan started as a ramification of the Nuvoletta clan, but has become independent and one of the most powerful groups of the Camorra over the time, surpassing the Nuvolettas

College Kidnappers.description: A Chicago-based group of University of Illinois alumni who kidnapped wealthy mobsters for ransom, active in the early 1930s. Led by Theodore 'Handsome Jack' Klutas, the group consisted of ten members. They targeted mobsters because they were unlikely to go to police and could pay ransoms.

descriptions: The College Kidnappers targeted members of the Al Capone mob for kidnapping.
edges.evidence_quote: Most of the victims snatched by The College Kidnappers were a part of the Al Capone mob.

descriptions: The Capone mob attempted to negotiate a payoff to stop the kidnappings, indicating a cooperative interaction.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1933, the Capone gangsters cornered Babe Jones, a member of The College Kidnappers, telling him to pass along the message of a payoff to stop kidnapping other Capone gangsters.

descriptions: Klutas, leader of the College Kidnappers, had connections with John Dillinger's gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Klutas had connections with John Dillinger and with the Chicago crime world.

Ghetto Brothers.description: The Ghetto Brothers were a gang and music group founded in New York City's South Bronx in the late 1960s. They were involved in politics, particularly the Puerto Rican independence movement, and aimed to uplift young Latino and Black men. The gang spread to much of the Northeastern United States and later merged with the Savage Nomads to form Los Solidos in Connecticut.

descriptions: Ghetto Brothers called a peace meeting attended by Black Spades.
edges.evidence_quote: It took place on December 8, 1971, and was attended by two hundred representatives from dozens of Bronx gangs, including the Black Pearls, Black Spades, Dirty Dozens, Dutchmen, Latin Spades, Liberated Panthers, Magnificent Seven, Peacemakers, Royal Javelins, Savage Skulls, Seven Immortals, Turbans, and Young Sinners, together with police and city officials.

descriptions: The Ghetto Brothers merged with Savage Nomads to create Los Solidos.
edges.evidence_quote: the Ghetto Brothers and the Savage Nomads joined to form Los Solidos

descriptions: Ghetto Brothers attempted to negotiate a truce with Mongols.
edges.evidence_quote: On December 2, 1971, Cornell Benjamin, known as "Black Benjie", a former heroin addict turned community activist who had joined the Bronx gang the Ghetto Brothers and become their minister of peace, led a delegation to meet with members of rival gangs the Mongols, the Savage Skulls, and the Seven Immortals in order to negotiate a truce.

descriptions: The Ghetto Brothers and Savage Nomads merged to form Los Solidos in the Connecticut prison system during the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: the Ghetto Brothers and the Savage Nomads joined to form Los Solidos

descriptions: Ghetto Brothers allowed the president of Savage Sisters to attend the peace meeting.
edges.evidence_quote: Only two women were permitted inside—the presidents of the all-girl gangs, the Alley Cats and the Savage Sisters—and they were seated in the last row, behind the warlords.

descriptions: Ghetto Brothers attempted to negotiate a truce with Savage Skulls.
edges.evidence_quote: On December 2, 1971, Cornell Benjamin, known as "Black Benjie", a former heroin addict turned community activist who had joined the Bronx gang the Ghetto Brothers and become their minister of peace, led a delegation to meet with members of rival gangs the Mongols, the Savage Skulls, and the Seven Immortals in order to negotiate a truce.

descriptions: Ghetto Brothers attempted to negotiate a truce with Seven Immortals.
edges.evidence_quote: On December 2, 1971, Cornell Benjamin, known as "Black Benjie", a former heroin addict turned community activist who had joined the Bronx gang the Ghetto Brothers and become their minister of peace, led a delegation to meet with members of rival gangs the Mongols, the Savage Skulls, and the Seven Immortals in order to negotiate a truce.

descriptions: Ghetto Brothers called a peace meeting attended by Turbans.
edges.evidence_quote: It took place on December 8, 1971, and was attended by two hundred representatives from dozens of Bronx gangs, including the Black Pearls, Black Spades, Dirty Dozens, Dutchmen, Latin Spades, Liberated Panthers, Magnificent Seven, Peacemakers, Royal Javelins, Savage Skulls, Seven Immortals, Turbans, and Young Sinners, together with police and city officials.

descriptions: Black Axe fought Maphite Confraternity in March 2010, resulting in nine deaths.
edges.evidence_quote: In March 2010, another fight broke out against the Maphite confraternity, resulting in the deaths of nine people.

descriptions: Cartel de Medellín and Autodefensas de Puerto Boyacá cooperated to train paramilitaries.
edges.evidence_quote: Gonzalo Rodríguez Gacha El Mexicano líder del Cártel de Medellín se entrevistó con Henry Pérez. Ambos acordaron en contratar instructores y mercenarios extranjeros para preparar a escoltas

Clan Galasso di Poggiomarino.description: The Clan Galasso di Poggiomarino was a Camorra organization operating in the hinterland east of Naples, based in Poggiomarino. Led by Pasquale Galasso and his brother Martino, it was considered one of the most powerful and ferocious clans in Camorra history, controlling neighboring municipalities and most public contracts in the province of Naples. The clan declined in the 1990s after Pasquale Galasso's arrest in 1992 and subsequent collaboration with justice.

descriptions: Galasso betrayed Alfieri, leading to his arrest.
edges.evidence_quote: Carmine Alfieri viene arrestato ... su soffiata del fidato Pasquale Galasso

descriptions: Alfieri clan allied with Galasso clan.
edges.evidence_quote: He also made new alliances with the Galasso clan from Poggiomarino

descriptions: Clan Alfieri allied with the Galasso clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan stringe nuove alleanze: con i Galasso di Poggiomarino

descriptions: Alfieri and Galasso became historical allies after Bardellino broke with Nuvoletta.
edges.evidence_quote: Bardellino, quando si rese conto del perché i Nuvoletta si mostrassero così attendisti e così poco propensi a chiudere la questione con Cutolo, si staccò definitivamente da loro, e legò con quelli che poi sarebbero diventati i suoi alleati storici, e cioè Carmine Alfieri e Pasquale Galasso.

descriptions: Galasso clan is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Galasso clan (defunct)

descriptions: The Galasso clan is listed as an ally of the Casalesi clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Galasso clan (defunct)

descriptions: The Galasso and Alfieri clans jointly organized a car bomb to kill Vincenzo Casillo.
edges.evidence_quote: autobomba organizzata dal clan Galasso e dal clan Alfieri

descriptions: The decline of Clan Galasso in the 1990s was inextricably linked to Clan Alfieri.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni novanta si assiste al declino del clan legato indissolubilmente ad un'altra grande realtà criminale, quella del clan Alfieri .

descriptions: Bardellino allied with Galasso after breaking with Nuvoletta.
edges.evidence_quote: Bardellino ... legò con quelli che poi sarebbero diventati i suoi alleati storici, e cioè Carmine Alfieri e Pasquale Galasso.

descriptions: Galasso participated in the killing of Ciro Nuvoletta.
edges.evidence_quote: Sicari del clan Alfieri-Galasso-Bardellino entrarono nella tenuta e spararono all'impazzata uccidendo Ciro Nuvoletta

descriptions: The Galasso clan killed NCO member Vincenzo Casillo in revenge for Casillo killing Nino Galasso.
edges.evidence_quote: autobomba organizzata dal clan Galasso e dal clan Alfieri come vendetta per l’omicidio commesso in precedenza da parte di Vincenzo Casillo nei confronti di Nino Galasso

descriptions: Clan Galasso expanded into Salerno province on behalf of Nuova Famiglia.
edges.evidence_quote: anche su interesse della Nuova Famiglia , ebbe un'estensione anche nella provincia di Salerno .

descriptions: Galasso was an ally of Bardellino, who was rival of Nuvoletta.
edges.evidence_quote: Bardellino appoggiato dai suoi alleati Carmine Alfieri e Pasquale Galasso

descriptions: The Galasso clan was a rival of the Nuvoletta clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Galasso clan (defunct)

descriptions: Both were members of the Nuova Famiglia federation.
edges.evidence_quote: the Nuova Famiglia (NF) was formed. The NF was a federation of Camorra clans which apart from the Nuovoletta clan, consisted of ... the Galasso clan

descriptions: Galasso clan is listed as an ally of Moccia clan in the infobox (defunct).
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Russo clan , Alfieri clan (defunct) , Licciardi clan , Galasso clan (defunct) , Sacco-Bocchetti clan , Contini clan

descriptions: Galasso clan was a member of the Nuova Famiglia confederation.
edges.evidence_quote: The NF consisted of Bardellino, Michele Zaza ... the Galasso clan

descriptions: Galasso joined the Nuova Famiglia.
edges.evidence_quote: Quando nella Nuova Famiglia subentrarono anche i Nuvoletta , gli Alfieri , i Galasso , i Misso della Sanità e soprattutto i Casalesi

descriptions: Nuova Famiglia allied with the Galasso clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Successivamente la FN si alleò alle cosche e ai boss vicini alla mafia siciliana, tra cui ... i Galasso

descriptions: The Galasso clan was part of the Nuova Famiglia confederation.
edges.evidence_quote: The confederation included: - Pasquale Galasso, head of the Galasso clan

descriptions: Mancuso supported the Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone group.
edges.evidence_quote: I Mancuso , i Piromalli e i Pesce appoggiavano il primo gruppo

descriptions: Pesce supported the Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone group.
edges.evidence_quote: I Mancuso , i Piromalli e i Pesce appoggiavano il primo gruppo

descriptions: Piromalli supported the Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone group.
edges.evidence_quote: I Mancuso , i Piromalli e i Pesce appoggiavano il primo gruppo

descriptions: Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone and Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino were in conflict in the Laureana di Borrello feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Laureana di Borrello : scaturisce all'inizio degli anni novanta tra le due fazioni Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone e i Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino

descriptions: These two groups were opposed in the Faida di Laureana in the 1980s.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni ottanta quando nella faida di Laureana di Borrello aveva visto contrapposte le cosche Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone e i Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino

descriptions: Amirante-Brunetti-Sibillo groups were aligned with Giuliano third generation.
edges.evidence_quote: The third generation of the Giulianos along with other small groups aligned to them, such as the Amirante-Brunetti-Sibillo, is dubbed by the media 'Paranza dei bambini'.

descriptions: Budvanska OCG is a regional clan within Škaljari OCG.
edges.evidence_quote: it operates as an association of highly independent regional clans - "Barska," OCG, "Budvanska" OCG, "Zemunska / Bojović" OCG.

descriptions: The Carvelli are allies of the Comberiati-Garofalo.
edges.evidence_quote: Sarebbero alleati dei Trovato e dei Comberiati-Garofalo di Petilia Policastro.

descriptions: Comberiati-Garofalo is listed as an allied 'ndrina in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: 'Ndrine alleate - Comberiati-Garofalo (Petilia Policastro)
